body.title: sudo relay: formalize as rack device, default-off, guru-start
body.result: SudoRelayDevice + SudoRelayShim + web server chat/feed endpoints; 14 tests; password never logged
body.intention: I intend that sudo relay is a first-class rack device with guru-only start and observable call output.
body.description:
sudo relay formalized as a rack device with defined lifecycle and constrained chat interface.

States:
- OFF: session does not exist
- NEEDPW: session running, waiting for operator to enter sudo password (chat window prompts for it)
- WAITING: password accepted, daemon loop running, watching for pending.sh
- PROCESSING: pending.sh present, currently executing a privileged command

Chat interface (slash-commands only, like Bark!):
- Accepts slash commands: /start, /stop, /status, /pw (to enter password in NEEDPW state)
- Free-text input: responds with 'Sorry nice person, no replies available for that.'
- No LLM, no cognition — purely mechanical command dispatch

Spec:
- Default state: OFF (does not start on rack boot)
- Start: guru-only (Akien manually starts it)
- Feed: public feed showing all call results (daemon.log contents)
- Must register with skeleton on start, appears in web device list with current state

Affected files: devices/sudo_relay/ (create shim.py + device.py), skeleton/registry.py, devices/web_server/server.py
Design rules: BaseDevice/BaseShim pattern; skeleton.registry.register() in shim.start(); no sqlite
Scope boundary: IN lifecycle + 4-state machine + registration + feed + slash-command chat. OUT: changing what the daemon executes
Completion criteria: (1) web device list shows sudo relay with correct state; (2) not started on rack boot; (3) slash commands work in chat; (4) free text gets canned response; (5) /pw command triggers password prompt in NEEDPW state; (6) feed shows daemon.log output

**HIGH-INERTIA APPROVAL (2026-06-05):** Akien approved via re-issuing /sprint-ticket twice. Security design decisions: (1) /pw command uses tmux send-keys directly, password never logged; (2) guru-only is convention-based, no hard enforcement at this stage.
